'''Bill Tupou''' (born 2 July 1990) ({{langx|to|Pila Tupou}}) is a former [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} or on the {{rlp|wg}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] and the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).

==Background==
Tupou was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand.

==Early years==
Tupou attended [[Kelston Boys High School]] and played for the [[Bay Roskill Vikings]] and [[Marist Saints]] in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition.

Tupou represented the [[New Zealand Rugby League|New Zealand]] Under-16 side and also represented [[Auckland rugby league team|Auckland]] Under-16 teams.<ref>[http://www.thewarriors.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-nyc-display&id=1525 Official profile] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090629090343/http://www.thewarriors.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-nyc-display&id=1525|date=29 June 2009}} ''Warriors.com.au''</ref>

==Playing career==
With the creation of the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]] in 2008 Tupou joined the [[New Zealand Warriors]] under-20 side and scored 14 tries in 19 games. He repeated this effort in 2009, scoring 14 tries in just 17 games.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/db/player/t/tupou_bill/index.php Bill TUPOU] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100210091956/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/t/tupou_bill/index.php|date=10 February 2010}} ''rleague.com''</ref>

Tupou was one of a small group of players still eligible for the side in 2010. In all, Tupou played 51 Toyota Cup games for the Junior Warriors, scoring thirty seven tries.<ref>[http://www.nzleague.co.nz/warriors/U20_player_profile.php?letter=T&id=22&name=Tupou, Bill Tupou]  {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111003230053/http://www.nzleague.co.nz/warriors/U20_player_profile.php?letter=T&id=22&name=Tupou%2C |date=3 October 2011}} ''nzleague.co.nz''</ref>

In 2010 he trained during the pre-season with the senior side and was named to make his [[National Rugby League|first grade]] début for the Warriors on 4 April 2010 against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]].<ref>[http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/536/Tupou-debut-Brown-back-to-face-Manly Tupou debut, Brown back to face Manly] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120225053843/http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/536/Tupou-debut-Brown-back-to-face-Manly|date=25 February 2012}} ''Vodafone Warriors News'', 30 March 2010</ref>

In 2010 he was picked for the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>[http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/60572/Default.aspx Nine NYC-winning Warriors named in Jnr Kiwis] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130173736/http://www.nrl.com/NewsViews/LatestNews/NewsArticle/tabid/10874/newsId/60572/Default.aspx |date=30 January 2016 }} ''NRL.com'', 6 October 2010</ref>

In the [[2011 NRL season]], Tupou played 17 games for the [[New Zealand Warriors]] as the club reached the [[2011 NRL Grand Final]].  Tupou played on the wing in the club's loss against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly-Warringah]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/nrl-grand-final-warriors-fall-short/NHCF3FV5WY254WRG2ZLOYGLHWY/|title=NRL Grand Final: Warriors fall short|website=www.nzherald.co.nz}}</ref>

On 12 October 2011 he was called into the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]] squad for the [[2011 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations tournament]].

In 2012, Tupou re-signed with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] until the end of the [[2014 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/nrl-winger-tupou-re-signs-with-warriors/CZZMECTOPBZMPZKK4U3AT4STX4/?c_id=4&objectid=10803036&ref=rss |title=NRL: Winger Tupou re-signs with Warriors |publisher=New Zealand Herald |date=2 May 2012 |access-date=21 December 2021}}</ref>

Tupou joined [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] on 25 June 2013. He had felt as though he was on the outer with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] under new coach Matt Elliot who had preferred others to him on the wing during the 2013 season.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/8837407/Tupou-leaves-Warriors-with-immediate-effect |title=Tupou leaves Warriors with immediate effect |author=David Long |publisher=Stuff |date=25 June 2013 |access-date=21 December 2021}}</ref>

Tupou joined [[Wakefield Trinity]] in July 2015 and was well known by Wakefield Trinity fans and Super League fans alike for his aggressive and fearless style of play as a powerful centre.  Tupou scored a memorable eleventh-minute hat trick in Wakefield's 42–30 win away at [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].  On 16 June 2021 he ruptured his patella tendon, resulting in surgery ending his 2021 campaign. Tupou announced on 5 July 2022 he would retire immediately due to complications from the previous injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bill-tupou-announces-retirement-with-immediate-effect/|title=Bill Tupou announces retirement with immediate effect|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web| url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/33597489| title=Bill Tupou joins Wakefield Wildcats from Canberra Raiders| publisher=bbc.co.uk| date=20 July 2015| access-date=23 July 2015}}</ref>
